---
title: Quarto configuration
---

Quarto configuration defines how source documents become rendered outputs.
For most course edits it is context to understand, not a file to modify.

## The main configuration file

Projects commonly place `_quarto.yml` next to their source documents. A
website may contain settings like:

```yaml
project:
  type: website
  output-dir: _site

website:
  title: "Course notes"
  sidebar:
    contents:
      - index.qmd
      - lessons/*.qmd

format:
  html:
    toc: true
```

Key responsibilities include:

| Configuration area | Purpose |
| --- | --- |
| `project.type` | Website project or document-oriented/default project |
| `project.output-dir` | Destination for rendered output |
| `project.render` | Which source documents should be built |
| `website` | Site title, navigation, search, sidebar, and links |
| `format` | HTML, notebook, PDF/Typst, and formatting options |
| `resources` | Files copied into published output |
| `filters` | Content transformations such as teaching-tools solution handling |

## Multiple output formats

A notebook/report project can request several formats:

```yaml
format:
  html:
    toc: true
  ipynb: default
  typst:
    toc: true
```

The exact formats and output folders are project choices. Update configuration
only when the required published product changes, not to make a normal prose
or exercise correction.

## Related configuration

For new projects with student and instructor outputs, install the teaching
tools and configure their filters in the base configuration:

```yaml
filters:
  - callout-solution
  - strip-solution
```

Profile files such as `_quarto-student.yml` then extend or override the base
configuration and set `teaching.show-solutions`. Extensions, filters, and
Pixi tasks may all influence the render; read them together when
investigating why a page appears differently from its source.
